---
title: "data"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```         
```

```{r read-data}
base_url <- "https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/cBioPortal/datahub/refs/heads/master/public/msk_chord_2024/"

clin_sample <- read.delim(
  paste0(base_url, "data_clinical_sample.txt"),
  comment.char = "#",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

mut <- read.delim(
  paste0(base_url, "data_mutations.txt"),
  comment.char = "#",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

cna <- read.delim(
  paste0(base_url, "data_cna.txt"),
  comment.char = "#",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)

sv <- read.delim(
  paste0(base_url, "data_sv.txt"),
  comment.char = "#",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
```

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Faster I/O for big files (mutations can be large)

library(data.table)

# Pretty table display in Rmd

library(knitr)
library(tidyverse)
Sys.setlocale("LC_CTYPE","en_US.UTF-8")
# ----- reproducibility + remote data config -----

set.seed(1)

base_url <- "https://media.githubusercontent.com/media/cBioPortal/datahub/refs/heads/master/public/msk_chord_2024/"
remote_path <- function(filename) paste0(base_url, filename)

# files used in this analysis are read directly from the MSK-CHORD GitHub repository
required_files <- c(
  "data_clinical_patient.txt",
  "data_clinical_sample.txt",
  "data_mutations.txt",
  "data_cna.txt",
  "data_sv.txt"
)


required_files

```

```{r load-clinical}

## 1) patient / sample 
patient_path <- remote_path("data_clinical_patient.txt")
sample_path  <- remote_path("data_clinical_sample.txt")

# cBioPortal text format usually starts with comment lines beginning with '#'
# readr::read_tsv(comment = "#") will ignore those lines
clinical_patient <- readr::read_tsv(patient_path, comment = "#", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  janitor::clean_names()

clinical_sample <- readr::read_tsv(sample_path, comment = "#", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  janitor::clean_names()

# basic overview
cat("clinical_patient rows:", nrow(clinical_patient), " cols:", ncol(clinical_patient), "\n")
cat("clinical_sample  rows:", nrow(clinical_sample),  " cols:", ncol(clinical_sample),  "\n")

# show column names
names(clinical_patient)
names(clinical_sample)

# peek
dplyr::glimpse(clinical_patient)
dplyr::glimpse(clinical_sample)

```

```{r clinical-qc}
# helper: missingness
miss_rate <- function(df) {
  tibble(
    col = names(df),
    missing_n = map_int(df, ~sum(is.na(.x) | .x == "")),
    missing_pct = missing_n / nrow(df)
  ) %>% arrange(desc(missing_pct))
}

patient_miss <- miss_rate(clinical_patient)
sample_miss  <- miss_rate(clinical_sample)

kable(head(patient_miss, 20), digits = 3, caption = "Top missing columns: patient")
kable(head(sample_miss, 20),  digits = 3, caption = "Top missing columns: sample")

# find likely OS / survival columns by name patterns
surv_candidates_patient <- names(clinical_patient)[str_detect(names(clinical_patient),
  "os|overall_survival|death|vital|days|months|survival|dfs|pfs|event"
)]
surv_candidates_sample <- names(clinical_sample)[str_detect(names(clinical_sample),
  "os|overall_survival|death|vital|days|months|survival|dfs|pfs|event"
)]

surv_candidates_patient
surv_candidates_sample

```

```{r join-patient-sample}
# 3)
# guess key columns
possible_patient_id_cols <- names(clinical_patient)[str_detect(names(clinical_patient), "patient.*id|patient_id")]
possible_sample_id_cols  <- names(clinical_sample)[str_detect(names(clinical_sample),  "sample.*id|sample_id")]
possible_link_cols       <- intersect(names(clinical_patient), names(clinical_sample))

possible_patient_id_cols
possible_sample_id_cols
head(possible_link_cols, 30)

# most cBioPortal datasets: patient_id in both; sample_id only in sample table
# adjust if your cleaned names differ
stopifnot("patient_id" %in% names(clinical_patient))
stopifnot("patient_id" %in% names(clinical_sample))

clinical_merged <- clinical_sample %>%
  left_join(clinical_patient, by = "patient_id", suffix = c("_sample", "_patient"))

cat("clinical_merged rows:", nrow(clinical_merged), " cols:", ncol(clinical_merged), "\n")
glimpse(clinical_merged)

```

```{r load-mutations}

mut_path <- remote_path("data_mutations.txt")

# Read directly from the MSK-CHORD GitHub raw file.
# cBioPortal text format starts with comment lines beginning with '#'.
mut <- readr::read_tsv(
  mut_path,
  comment = "#",
  show_col_types = FALSE
) |>
  janitor::clean_names()

cat("Read mutations directly from GitHub raw URL.\n")
cat("mut rows:", nrow(mut), " cols:", ncol(mut), "\n")

names(mut)
dplyr::glimpse(mut)

mut_cols_guess <- names(mut)[stringr::str_detect(names(mut),
  "hugo|gene|sample|tumor|patient|variant|class|protein|effect|oncokb|tmb"
)]
mut_cols_guess

mut |>
  dplyr::count(hugo_symbol, sort = TRUE) |>
  head(20) |>
  knitr::kable()
```

```{r}
mut_samples <- unique(mut$tumor_sample_barcode)
clin_samples <- unique(clinical_sample$sample_id)

# overlap check
cat("Unique mut samples:", length(mut_samples), "\n")
cat("Unique clinical samples:", length(clin_samples), "\n")
cat("Overlap samples:", length(intersect(mut_samples, clin_samples)), "\n")

# how many mutation samples NOT in clinical_sample?
cat("Mut samples missing in clinical_sample:", length(setdiff(mut_samples, clin_samples)), "\n")

# and vice versa
cat("Clinical samples missing in mutations:", length(setdiff(clin_samples, mut_samples)), "\n")

```

```{r load-cna-sv}
# helper: read a cBioPortal TSV while skipping leading/comment lines beginning with "#"
# Use base::read.delim instead of readr::read_tsv here because data_cna.txt has
# very long rows and readr/vroom can fail with a connection-buffer error.
fread_cbio <- function(path, sep = "\t") {
  read.delim(
    path,
    sep = sep,
    comment.char = "#",
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
    check.names = FALSE
  ) |>
    janitor::clean_names()
}

# ---- read CNA / SV ----
cna_path <- remote_path("data_cna.txt")
sv_path  <- remote_path("data_sv.txt")

cna <- fread_cbio(cna_path)
sv  <- fread_cbio(sv_path)

cat("cna rows:", nrow(cna), " cols:", ncol(cna), "\n")
cat("sv  rows:", nrow(sv),  " cols:", ncol(sv),  "\n")

# show a few column names to understand the schema
names(cna)[1:min(30, ncol(cna))]
names(sv)

dplyr::glimpse(sv)

```

```{r load-timelines}
timeline_names <- c("treatment", "progression", "tumor_sites")
timeline_files <- setNames(
  paste0(base_url, "data_timeline_", timeline_names, ".txt"),
  timeline_names
)

timeline_list <- timeline_files %>%
  map(~ readr::read_tsv(.x, comment = "#", show_col_types = FALSE) %>% janitor::clean_names())

# list what you have
names(timeline_list)

# for each timeline table: show size + key columns
timeline_summary <- imap_dfr(timeline_list, ~ tibble(
  timeline = .y,
  n = nrow(.x),
  p = ncol(.x),
  cols = paste(names(.x)[1:min(12, ncol(.x))], collapse = ", ")
))

kable(timeline_summary, caption = "Timeline tables overview")

# peek a couple of the most relevant
glimpse(timeline_list$treatment)
glimpse(timeline_list$progression)
glimpse(timeline_list$tumor_sites)

```

```{r find-cols}
find_cols <- function(df, pattern) {
  names(df)[str_detect(names(df), pattern)]
}

find_cols(clinical_patient, "cancer|tumor|primary|site|hist|diagnos")
find_cols(clinical_patient, "stage|ajcc|tnm|grade")
find_cols(clinical_patient, "sex|gender|age|race|ethnic")
find_cols(clinical_patient, "os|overall|survival|death|vital|status|event|days|months")

find_cols(mut, "sample|tumor_sample|barcode|patient")

```

```{r}
library(stringr)

# --- normalize sample id across tables ---
normalize_sample_id <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  x <- x[!is.na(x) & x != ""]
  x <- str_trim(x)
  x <- str_to_upper(x)

  # clean_names() often turns "P-0001-T01-IM6" -> "p_0001_t01_im6"
  x <- str_replace_all(x, "_", "-")
  x <- str_replace(x, "^P-", "P-")      # no-op but safe
  x <- str_replace(x, "^P-", "P-")      # keep consistent
  x <- str_replace(x, "^P-", "P-")      # (safe)
  x <- str_replace(x, "^P-", "P-")      # (safe)
  x <- str_replace(x, "^P-", "P-")      # (safe)

  # key fix: P_XXXX... -> P-XXXX...
  x <- str_replace(x, "^P-", "P-")      # after underscore->dash might already be P-...
  x <- str_replace(x, "^P-", "P-")      # keep stable
  x <- str_replace(x, "^P-", "P-")

  # actually important one:
  x <- str_replace(x, "^P-", "P-")
  x <- str_replace(x, "^P-", "P-")
  x <- str_replace(x, "^P-", "P-")

  # simplest: ensure prefix is P- (covers P_ -> P- after "_"->"-")
  x <- str_replace(x, "^P-", "P-")
  x <- str_replace(x, "^P-", "P-")

  unique(x)
}

# NOTE: the repeated str_replace("^P-") lines are harmless but redundant.
# If you want it cleaner, keep only the 2 lines below:
# x <- str_replace_all(x, "_", "-")
# x <- str_replace(x, "^P-", "P-")

# --- extract & normalize ids ---
clinical_ids <- normalize_sample_id(clinical_sample$sample_id)
mut_ids      <- normalize_sample_id(mut$tumor_sample_barcode)

cna_ids_raw <- colnames(cna)
cna_ids_raw <- cna_ids_raw[cna_ids_raw != "hugo_symbol"]
cna_ids     <- normalize_sample_id(cna_ids_raw)

sv_ids      <- normalize_sample_id(sv$sample_id)

# --- overlaps (debug-friendly) ---
overlap_all <- Reduce(intersect, list(clinical_ids, mut_ids, cna_ids, sv_ids))
cat("Overlap all 4:", length(overlap_all), "\n")

cat("Pairwise overlaps:\n")
cat("clinical ∩ mut:", length(intersect(clinical_ids, mut_ids)), "\n")
cat("clinical ∩ cna:", length(intersect(clinical_ids, cna_ids)), "\n")
cat("clinical ∩ sv :", length(intersect(clinical_ids, sv_ids)), "\n")
cat("mut ∩ cna     :", length(intersect(mut_ids, cna_ids)), "\n")
cat("mut ∩ sv      :", length(intersect(mut_ids, sv_ids)), "\n")
cat("cna ∩ sv      :", length(intersect(cna_ids, sv_ids)), "\n")

# sanity: show a few examples
cat("\nExamples:\n")
print(head(clinical_ids, 3))
print(head(cna_ids, 3))
print(head(sv_ids, 3))
```

```{r}
library(tibble)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)


df_avail <- tibble(
  dataset = c("Clinical samples", "Mutation samples", "CNA samples", "SV samples",
              "Overlap (Clinical ∩ Mut ∩ CNA ∩ SV)"),
  n = c(length(clinical_ids),
        length(mut_ids),
        length(cna_ids),
        length(sv_ids),
        length(overlap_all))
) %>%
  mutate(dataset = factor(dataset, levels = dataset))  # keep plotting order

p1 <- ggplot(df_avail, aes(x = dataset, y = n)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.4, size = 4) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))) +
  labs(
    title = "Data availability (unique samples)",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1)
  )

p1

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

analysis_samples <- overlap_all  # 5134 个 sample_id

df_onco <- clinical_sample %>%
  filter(sample_id %in% analysis_samples) %>%
  count(oncotree_code, sort = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    oncotree_code = ifelse(is.na(oncotree_code) | oncotree_code == "", "Missing", oncotree_code)
  ) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15) %>%
  mutate(oncotree_code = reorder(oncotree_code, n))

p2a <- ggplot(df_onco, aes(x = oncotree_code, y = n)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.15, size = 4) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    title = "Cohort composition (Top OncoTree codes)",
    subtitle = paste0("n = ", length(analysis_samples), " samples with Clinical ∩ Mut ∩ CNA ∩ SV"),
    x = NULL,
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10)
  )

p2a

```

```{r}
df_meta <- clinical_sample %>%
  filter(sample_id %in% analysis_samples) %>%
  mutate(
    metastatic_site = ifelse(is.na(metastatic_site) | metastatic_site == "", "Missing", metastatic_site)
  ) %>%
  count(metastatic_site, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15) %>%
  mutate(metastatic_site = reorder(metastatic_site, n))

p2b <- ggplot(df_meta, aes(x = metastatic_site, y = n)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.15, size = 4) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    title = "Metastatic site distribution (Top sites)",
    subtitle = paste0("n = ", length(analysis_samples), " samples with Clinical ∩ Mut ∩ CNA ∩ SV"),
    x = NULL,
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10)
  )

p2b

```

```{r fig3-pca, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# =========================
# Fig 3: Feature construction (Mut + CNA + SV) + PCA visualization
# Cohort: overlap_all (your ~5134 samples)
# =========================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(ggplot2)
})

stopifnot(exists("overlap_all"), exists("clinical_sample"), exists("mut"), exists("cna"), exists("sv"))

# -------------------------
# Step 0) cohort + labels
# -------------------------
analysis_ids <- str_to_upper(overlap_all)

label_df <- clinical_sample %>%
  mutate(sample_id_std = str_to_upper(sample_id)) %>%
  filter(sample_id_std %in% analysis_ids) %>%
  transmute(
    sample_id_std,
    oncotree_code = ifelse(is.na(oncotree_code) | oncotree_code == "", "Missing", as.character(oncotree_code)),
    metastatic_site = ifelse(is.na(metastatic_site) | metastatic_site == "", "Missing", as.character(metastatic_site))
  )

cat("Cohort size:", length(analysis_ids), "\n")

# -------------------------
# Step 1) MUT features (binary presence of top mutated genes)
# -------------------------
mut_std <- mut %>%
  transmute(
    sample_id_std = str_to_upper(tumor_sample_barcode),
    gene = hugo_symbol
  ) %>%
  filter(sample_id_std %in% analysis_ids) %>%
  filter(!is.na(gene), gene != "") %>%
  distinct(sample_id_std, gene)

top_mut_genes <- mut_std %>%
  count(gene, sort = TRUE, name = "n_samples") %>%
  slice_head(n = 200) %>%              # can change to 100 if you want faster
  pull(gene)

mut_wide <- mut_std %>%
  filter(gene %in% top_mut_genes) %>%
  mutate(value = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = gene, values_from = value, values_fill = 0)

mut_wide2 <- tibble(sample_id_std = analysis_ids) %>%
  left_join(mut_wide, by = "sample_id_std") %>%
  mutate(across(-sample_id_std, ~replace_na(.x, 0))) %>%
  arrange(match(sample_id_std, analysis_ids))

mut_X <- as.matrix(select(mut_wide2, -sample_id_std))
colnames(mut_X) <- paste0("MUT_", make.names(colnames(mut_X)))
rownames(mut_X) <- mut_wide2$sample_id_std

cat("MUT matrix:", nrow(mut_X), "x", ncol(mut_X), "\n")

# -------------------------
# Step 2) CNA features (top variable genes)
# -------------------------
cna_cols_raw <- setdiff(names(cna), "hugo_symbol")

# map CNA column name -> standardized sample id
cna_map <- tibble(cna_col = cna_cols_raw) %>%
  mutate(sample_id_std = str_to_upper(cna_col) %>% str_replace_all("_", "-")) %>%
  filter(sample_id_std %in% analysis_ids)

cat("CNA columns in cohort:", nrow(cna_map), "\n")

# subset cna to cohort columns only
cna_sub <- cna %>%
  select(hugo_symbol, all_of(cna_map$cna_col))

# convert to numeric matrix (genes x samples)
cna_gene <- cna_sub$hugo_symbol
cna_M <- as.matrix(cna_sub[, -1, drop = FALSE])

# force numeric; anything non-numeric becomes NA -> later handled
cna_M <- apply(cna_M, 2, function(x) as.numeric(x))

# choose top variable genes (ignore NA)
gene_var <- apply(cna_M, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE)
gene_var[is.na(gene_var)] <- 0

top_idx <- order(gene_var, decreasing = TRUE)[1:300]   # can reduce to 200 for speed
cna_M2 <- cna_M[top_idx, , drop = FALSE]
cna_gene2 <- cna_gene[top_idx]

# transpose => samples x genes
cna_X_raw <- t(cna_M2)
rownames(cna_X_raw) <- cna_map$sample_id_std
colnames(cna_X_raw) <- paste0("CNA_", make.names(cna_gene2))

# fill full cohort order (if a cohort sample missing CNA column -> zeros)
cna_X <- matrix(0, nrow = length(analysis_ids), ncol = ncol(cna_X_raw))
rownames(cna_X) <- analysis_ids
colnames(cna_X) <- colnames(cna_X_raw)
cna_X[rownames(cna_X_raw), ] <- cna_X_raw

cat("CNA matrix:", nrow(cna_X), "x", ncol(cna_X), "\n")

# -------------------------
# Step 3) SV features (total + top classes)
# -------------------------
sv_std <- sv %>%
  transmute(
    sample_id_std = str_to_upper(sample_id),
    class = ifelse(is.na(class) | class == "", "UNKNOWN", as.character(class))
  ) %>%
  filter(sample_id_std %in% analysis_ids)

top_sv_classes <- sv_std %>%
  count(class, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  pull(class)

sv_class <- sv_std %>%
  filter(class %in% top_sv_classes) %>%
  count(sample_id_std, class, name = "n") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = class, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)

sv_total <- sv_std %>%
  count(sample_id_std, name = "SV_total")

sv_wide2 <- tibble(sample_id_std = analysis_ids) %>%
  left_join(sv_total, by = "sample_id_std") %>%
  left_join(sv_class, by = "sample_id_std") %>%
  mutate(across(-sample_id_std, ~replace_na(.x, 0))) %>%
  arrange(match(sample_id_std, analysis_ids))

sv_X <- as.matrix(select(sv_wide2, -sample_id_std))
colnames(sv_X) <- paste0("SV_", make.names(colnames(sv_X)))
rownames(sv_X) <- sv_wide2$sample_id_std

cat("SV matrix:", nrow(sv_X), "x", ncol(sv_X), "\n")

# -------------------------
# Step 4) Combine + CLEAN (this is where svd error is fixed)
# -------------------------
X <- cbind(mut_X, cna_X, sv_X)

# 4.1 replace NA/Inf with 0
X[!is.finite(X)] <- NA
X[is.na(X)] <- 0

# 4.2 drop zero-variance columns (sd=0 => scale gives NaN)
col_sd <- apply(X, 2, sd)
keep <- which(is.finite(col_sd) & col_sd > 0)
X2 <- X[, keep, drop = FALSE]

cat("Combined X:", nrow(X), "x", ncol(X), "\n")
cat("After drop sd=0 cols:", nrow(X2), "x", ncol(X2), "\n")

# 4.3 scale
X_scaled <- scale(X2)

# final sanity
stopifnot(all(is.finite(X_scaled)))

```

```{r}
# =========================
# Next step (for clustering + association later):
# Build a clean sample-level label table from clinical_sample
# Cohort: analysis_ids (= upper-case overlap_all)
# =========================

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

stopifnot(exists("analysis_ids"), exists("clinical_sample"))

# helper: pick an existing column name (in case MSK-CHORD uses slightly different naming)
pick_col <- function(df, candidates) {
  cand <- candidates[candidates %in% names(df)]
  if (length(cand) == 0) return(NA_character_)
  cand[[1]]
}

# try common column names (after janitor::clean_names)
col_oncotree <- pick_col(clinical_sample, c("oncotree_code", "oncotree", "oncotree_code_sample"))
col_primary  <- pick_col(clinical_sample, c("primary_site", "primary_site_sample", "primary_site_description"))
col_meta     <- pick_col(clinical_sample, c("metastatic_site", "metastatic_site_sample"))
col_tmb      <- pick_col(clinical_sample, c("tmb_nonsynonymous", "tmb", "tmb_score", "tmb_nonsyn"))
col_msi      <- pick_col(clinical_sample, c("msi_type", "msi_status", "msi"))

cat("Using columns:\n",
    "oncotree:", col_oncotree, "\n",
    "primary :", col_primary,  "\n",
    "metast  :", col_meta,     "\n",
    "tmb     :", col_tmb,      "\n",
    "msi     :", col_msi,      "\n")

clinical_sample_std <- clinical_sample %>%
  mutate(sample_id_std = str_to_upper(sample_id))

# label table (keep it simple + clean)
sample_labels <- clinical_sample_std %>%
  filter(sample_id_std %in% analysis_ids) %>%
  transmute(
    sample_id_std,
    oncotree_code   = if (!is.na(col_oncotree)) as.character(.data[[col_oncotree]]) else NA_character_,
    primary_site    = if (!is.na(col_primary))  as.character(.data[[col_primary]])  else NA_character_,
    metastatic_site = if (!is.na(col_meta))     as.character(.data[[col_meta]])     else NA_character_,
    tmb             = if (!is.na(col_tmb))      suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[col_tmb]])) else NA_real_,
    msi             = if (!is.na(col_msi))      as.character(.data[[col_msi]])      else NA_character_
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    oncotree_code   = ifelse(is.na(oncotree_code)   | oncotree_code   == "", "Missing", oncotree_code),
    primary_site    = ifelse(is.na(primary_site)    | primary_site    == "", "Missing", primary_site),
    metastatic_site = ifelse(is.na(metastatic_site) | metastatic_site == "", "Missing", metastatic_site),
    msi             = ifelse(is.na(msi)             | msi             == "", "Missing", msi)
  ) %>%
  distinct(sample_id_std, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  arrange(match(sample_id_std, analysis_ids))

# quick sanity
stopifnot(nrow(sample_labels) == length(analysis_ids))
dplyr::glimpse(sample_labels)

# how much missing in key biomarkers?
cat("\nMissingness in cohort:\n")
cat("TMB missing:", sum(is.na(sample_labels$tmb)), "/", nrow(sample_labels), "\n")
cat("MSI missing:", sum(sample_labels$msi == "Missing"), "/", nrow(sample_labels), "\n")
```

```{r}
# =========================
# Next step:
# Clean / harmonize clinical labels for downstream association tests
# - Collapse rare metastatic sites

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(forcats)

stopifnot(exists("sample_labels"))

lab2 <- sample_labels %>%
  mutate(
    # --- clean metastatic_site strings (tiny normalization) ---
    metastatic_site = str_squish(metastatic_site),
    metastatic_site = str_replace_all(metastatic_site, "Lymph node", "Lymph Node"),

    # --- collapse metastatic_site into Top K + Other ---
    meta_top = fct_lump_n(factor(metastatic_site), n = 8, w = NULL, other_level = "Other"),

    # --- MSI: keep raw + derive a simple binary (you can refine later) ---
    msi_raw = msi,
    msi_bin = case_when(
      str_detect(str_to_lower(msi_raw), "high|unstable") ~ "MSI-High/Unstable",
      str_detect(str_to_lower(msi_raw), "stable")       ~ "MSS/Stable",
      TRUE                                              ~ "Other/Unknown"
    ),
    msi_bin = factor(msi_bin, levels = c("MSS/Stable", "MSI-High/Unstable", "Other/Unknown")),

    # --- TMB: keep continuous + make a simple 3-bin version for quick plots ---
    tmb = as.numeric(tmb),
    tmb_group = ntile(tmb, 3) %>% factor(labels = c("Low", "Mid", "High"))
  )

# quick checks
cat("Metastatic site (Top 8 + Other):\n")
print(table(lab2$meta_top))

cat("\nMSI_bin counts:\n")
print(table(lab2$msi_bin, useNA = "ifany"))

# save for later cells
sample_labels2 <- lab2

dplyr::glimpse(sample_labels2)
```

```{r}
# =========================
# PCA dimension choice (practical)
# - In high-dim genomic features, variance spreads across many PCs.
# - For clustering, use a fixed PC dimension (e.g., 50) and later interpret clusters in original features.
# =========================

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

stopifnot(exists("X_scaled"), exists("analysis_ids"), exists("sample_labels2"))

pca <- prcomp(X_scaled, center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE)

var_explained <- (pca$sdev^2) / sum(pca$sdev^2)
cumvar <- cumsum(var_explained)

# Plot cumulative variance for first 200 PCs
df_var <- tibble(
  PC = seq_along(cumvar),
  cumvar = cumvar
)

ggplot(df_var %>% slice(1:200), aes(PC, cumvar)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 1.0) +
  labs(title = "Cumulative variance explained (first 200 PCs)",
       x = "PC index", y = "Cumulative variance") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

# Choose a practical PC dimension for clustering
pc_dim <- 50   # try 50 first; later sensitivity: 30 / 80 / 100
cat("Using PC_dim =", pc_dim,
    " | cumvar@PC_dim =", round(cumvar[pc_dim], 3), "\n")

Z_clust <- pca$x[, 1:pc_dim, drop = FALSE]
stopifnot(all(is.finite(Z_clust)))

# For plotting (PC1/PC2)
pca_df <- tibble(
  sample_id_std = toupper(analysis_ids),
  PC1 = pca$x[, 1],
  PC2 = pca$x[, 2]
) %>%
  left_join(sample_labels2 %>% mutate(sample_id_std = toupper(sample_id_std)),
            by = "sample_id_std")

pca_obj <- pca
```

```{r}
# =========================
# Step: k-means clustering on PCA space (Z_clust)
# - We'll scan K=2..10
# - Use (1) elbow: total within-cluster sum of squares (WSS)
# - Use (2) silhouette: average silhouette width
# =========================

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

stopifnot(exists("Z_clust"), exists("pca_df"))

set.seed(1)

K_grid <- 2:10

# ---- elbow (WSS) ----
wss <- sapply(K_grid, function(k) {
  km <- kmeans(Z_clust, centers = k, nstart = 20, iter.max = 100)
  km$tot.withinss
})

df_wss <- tibble(K = K_grid, WSS = wss)

p_elbow <- ggplot(df_wss, aes(K, WSS)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 1.6) +
  labs(title = "Elbow plot (k-means on PCA space)", x = "K", y = "Total within-cluster SS") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_elbow)

# ---- silhouette (requires cluster package) ----
if (!requireNamespace("cluster", quietly = TRUE)) {
  message("Package 'cluster' not installed. Run: install.packages('cluster') to compute silhouette.")
} else {
  avg_sil <- sapply(K_grid, function(k) {
    km <- kmeans(Z_clust, centers = k, nstart = 20, iter.max = 100)
    sil <- cluster::silhouette(km$cluster, dist(Z_clust))
    mean(sil[, "sil_width"])
  })
  
  df_sil <- tibble(K = K_grid, avg_silhouette = avg_sil)
  
  p_sil <- ggplot(df_sil, aes(K, avg_silhouette)) +
    geom_line() + geom_point(size = 1.6) +
    labs(title = "Silhouette (k-means on PCA space)", x = "K", y = "Average silhouette width") +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12)
  
  print(p_sil)
}

# ---- pick a default K for next step ----
# (We'll revise after seeing the plots)
K_default <- 5
cat("Default K for next step:", K_default, "\n")

km_final <- kmeans(Z_clust, centers = K_default, nstart = 50, iter.max = 200)

# attach cluster labels to pca_df (for visualization)
pca_df2 <- pca_df %>%
  mutate(cluster = factor(km_final$cluster))

# quick PCA scatter colored by cluster
p_cluster <- ggplot(pca_df2, aes(PC1, PC2, color = cluster)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1.2) +
  labs(title = paste0("PCA scatter colored by k-means clusters (K=", K_default, ")"),
       x = "PC1", y = "PC2", color = "Cluster") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

print(p_cluster)

# keep for next cells
km_k <- K_default
km_fit <- km_final
pca_with_cluster <- pca_df2
```

```{r}
# =========================
# Step: Run k-means on PCA space (Z_clust) and attach cluster labels
# Inputs expected from previous cells: Z_clust, analysis_ids, pca_df, sample_labels2
# =========================
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)

stopifnot(exists("Z_clust"), exists("analysis_ids"), exists("pca_df"))

K <- 5  # <- change if you want (based on your elbow/silhouette)
set.seed(1)
km <- kmeans(Z_clust, centers = K, nstart = 50, iter.max = 200)

cluster_df <- tibble(
  sample_id_std = analysis_ids,
  cluster = factor(km$cluster)
)

# quick size table
cluster_sizes <- cluster_df %>%
  count(cluster, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(desc(n))
kable(cluster_sizes, caption = paste0("Cluster sizes (K=", K, ")"))

# PCA scatter colored by clusters (for slides)
p_pca_km <- pca_df %>%
  left_join(cluster_df, by = "sample_id_std") %>%
  ggplot(aes(PC1, PC2, color = cluster)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1.2) +
  labs(
    title = paste0("PCA scatter colored by k-means clusters (K=", K, ")"),
    subtitle = paste0("n = ", length(analysis_ids), " samples (overlap cohort)"),
    x = "PC1", y = "PC2", color = "Cluster"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

p_pca_km

# keep for next steps
km_obj <- km
cluster_assignments <- cluster_df
```

```{r}
# =========================
# Step: Clinical association checks (Cluster vs OncoTree / primary / metastatic / TMB / MSI)
# Inputs expected: cluster_assignments, sample_labels2  (your 5134 x 6 table)
# =========================
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)

stopifnot(exists("cluster_assignments"), exists("sample_labels2"))

df <- sample_labels2 %>%
  left_join(cluster_assignments, by = "sample_id_std") %>%
  mutate(
    cluster = factor(cluster),
    oncotree_code   = ifelse(is.na(oncotree_code) | oncotree_code == "", "Missing", oncotree_code),
    primary_site    = ifelse(is.na(primary_site) | primary_site == "", "Missing", primary_site),
    metastatic_site = ifelse(is.na(metastatic_site) | metastatic_site == "", "Missing", metastatic_site),
    msi             = ifelse(is.na(msi) | msi == "", "Missing", msi)
  )

# -------------------------
# 1) Cluster vs OncoTree (chi-square)
# -------------------------
tab_onco <- table(df$cluster, df$oncotree_code)
chi_onco <- chisq.test(tab_onco)

cat("Chi-square test: cluster vs oncotree_code\n")
cat("p-value:", signif(chi_onco$p.value, 3), "\n\n")

# show top OncoTree composition by cluster (top 8 per cluster)
onco_top <- df %>%
  count(cluster, oncotree_code, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  arrange(cluster, desc(n)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 8) %>%
  ungroup()

kable(onco_top, digits = 3, caption = "Top OncoTree codes within each cluster (top 8)")

# quick stacked bar (top 12 oncotree overall)
top12_onco <- df %>% count(oncotree_code, sort = TRUE) %>% slice_head(n = 12) %>% pull(oncotree_code)
p_onco <- df %>%
  mutate(oncotree_top = ifelse(oncotree_code %in% top12_onco, oncotree_code, "Other")) %>%
  count(cluster, oncotree_top) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = cluster, y = pct, fill = oncotree_top)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(title = "Cluster composition by OncoTree (top 12 + Other)", x = "Cluster", y = "Within-cluster proportion", fill = "OncoTree") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)
p_onco

# -------------------------
# 2) Cluster vs Metastatic site (chi-square)
# -------------------------
tab_meta <- table(df$cluster, df$metastatic_site)
chi_meta <- chisq.test(tab_meta)

cat("\nChi-square test: cluster vs metastatic_site\n")
cat("p-value:", signif(chi_meta$p.value, 3), "\n")

# top metastatic sites per cluster (top 8)
meta_top <- df %>%
  count(cluster, metastatic_site, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  arrange(cluster, desc(n)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 8) %>%
  ungroup()

kable(meta_top, digits = 3, caption = "Top metastatic sites within each cluster (top 8)")

# -------------------------
# 3) Cluster vs TMB (boxplot + Kruskal-Wallis)
# -------------------------
# (TMB is continuous; use non-parametric test for safety)
kw_tmb <- kruskal.test(tmb ~ cluster, data = df)

cat("\nKruskal-Wallis: TMB across clusters\n")
cat("p-value:", signif(kw_tmb$p.value, 3), "\n")

p_tmb <- ggplot(df, aes(x = cluster, y = tmb)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.25) +
  labs(title = "TMB distribution by cluster", x = "Cluster", y = "TMB (nonsynonymous)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)
p_tmb

# -------------------------
# 4) Cluster vs MSI (chi-square)
# -------------------------
df_msi <- df %>% filter(msi != "Do not report")  # comment out if you want to keep it
tab_msi <- table(df_msi$cluster, df_msi$msi)
chi_msi <- chisq.test(tab_msi)

cat("\nChi-square test: cluster vs MSI (excluding 'Do not report')\n")
cat("p-value:", signif(chi_msi$p.value, 3), "\n")

# keep results for report
assoc_results <- list(
  chi_onco = chi_onco,
  chi_meta = chi_meta,
  kw_tmb   = kw_tmb,
  chi_msi  = chi_msi
)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

stopifnot(exists("df"))

p_meta <- df %>%
  mutate(meta_plot = as.character(meta_top)) %>%
  count(cluster, meta_plot) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = cluster, y = pct, fill = meta_plot)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "Cluster composition by metastatic site (Top 8 + Other)",
    x = "Cluster", y = "Within-cluster proportion", fill = "Metastatic site"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

p_meta
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(knitr)

stopifnot(exists("analysis_ids"), exists("mut"), exists("cna"), exists("sv"))
stopifnot(exists("km"))  
cluster_df <- tibble(
  sample_id_std = analysis_ids,
  cluster = factor(km$cluster)
)

# quick sanity
kable(cluster_df %>% count(cluster, name="n") %>% arrange(desc(n)),
      caption = "Cluster sizes (from km$cluster)")
```

```{r}
ls()
```

```{r}
# =========================
# Next step (Final project): Interpret clusters biologically
# 1) Top mutated genes per cluster (within-cluster frequency)
# 2) CNA burden per sample (FULL 702 genes) + by-cluster boxplots
# 3) SV class patterns by cluster (mean counts)
# Requires existing objects: analysis_ids, mut, cna, sv, cluster_assignments
# =========================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(forcats)
  library(scales)
})

stopifnot(exists("analysis_ids"), exists("mut"), exists("cna"), exists("sv"), exists("cluster_assignments"))

# Ensure cluster table exists and is aligned
cluster_df <- cluster_assignments %>%
  mutate(sample_id_std = str_to_upper(sample_id_std),
         cluster = factor(cluster)) %>%
  arrange(match(sample_id_std, str_to_upper(analysis_ids)))

stopifnot(nrow(cluster_df) == length(analysis_ids))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# (A) Top mutated genes per cluster (within-cluster frequency)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
mut_long <- mut %>%
  transmute(
    sample_id_std = str_to_upper(tumor_sample_barcode),
    gene = hugo_symbol
  ) %>%
  filter(sample_id_std %in% str_to_upper(analysis_ids),
         !is.na(gene), gene != "") %>%
  distinct(sample_id_std, gene) %>%
  inner_join(cluster_df, by = "sample_id_std")

cluster_sizes <- cluster_df %>% count(cluster, name = "cluster_n")

# Top 10 genes within each cluster by pct of samples mutated
top_genes_by_cluster <- mut_long %>%
  count(cluster, gene, name = "n_mut_samples") %>%
  left_join(cluster_sizes, by = "cluster") %>%
  mutate(pct = n_mut_samples / cluster_n) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  arrange(desc(pct), desc(n_mut_samples)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  ungroup()

# Plot: faceted bars (pct within cluster)
p_mut <- top_genes_by_cluster %>%
  mutate(gene = fct_reorder(gene, pct)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = pct, y = gene)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75) +
  facet_wrap(~ cluster, scales = "free_y", nrow = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Top mutated genes by cluster (within-cluster frequency)",
    x = "% of samples in cluster with a mutation in gene",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_mut)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# (B) CNA burden (FULL 702 genes) + by-cluster boxplots
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Map CNA columns to standardized sample IDs
cna_cols_raw <- setdiff(names(cna), "hugo_symbol")
cna_map_full <- tibble(cna_col = cna_cols_raw) %>%
  mutate(sample_id_std = str_to_upper(cna_col) %>% str_replace_all("_", "-")) %>%
  filter(sample_id_std %in% str_to_upper(analysis_ids))

# Subset CNA to overlap cohort columns only
cna_full <- cna %>% select(hugo_symbol, all_of(cna_map_full$cna_col))

# Convert to numeric matrix (genes x samples). 702 x 5134-ish
cna_M_full <- as.matrix(cna_full[, -1, drop = FALSE])
cna_M_full <- apply(cna_M_full, 2, suppressWarnings(as.numeric))
cna_M_full[!is.finite(cna_M_full)] <- NA

# Burden per sample (over 702 genes)
cna_burden <- tibble(
  cna_col = colnames(cna_M_full),
  amp_n   = colSums(cna_M_full > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
  del_n   = colSums(cna_M_full < 0, na.rm = TRUE),
  abs_sum = colSums(abs(cna_M_full), na.rm = TRUE)
) %>%
  left_join(cna_map_full, by = "cna_col") %>%
  select(sample_id_std, amp_n, del_n, abs_sum) %>%
  right_join(cluster_df, by = "sample_id_std")

# Boxplots
p_cna_amp <- ggplot(cna_burden, aes(x = cluster, y = amp_n)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.2) +
  labs(title = "CNA burden by cluster: #amplified genes (>0)", x = "Cluster", y = "Amp count") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

p_cna_del <- ggplot(cna_burden, aes(x = cluster, y = del_n)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.2) +
  labs(title = "CNA burden by cluster: #deleted genes (<0)", x = "Cluster", y = "Del count") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

p_cna_abs <- ggplot(cna_burden, aes(x = cluster, y = abs_sum)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.2) +
  labs(title = "CNA burden by cluster: sum(abs(CNA)) across 702 genes", x = "Cluster", y = "Sum abs(CNA)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_cna_amp)
print(p_cna_del)
print(p_cna_abs)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# (C) SV class patterns by cluster (mean counts per sample)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
sv_long <- sv %>%
  transmute(
    sample_id_std = str_to_upper(sample_id),
    class = ifelse(is.na(class) | class == "", "UNKNOWN", as.character(class))
  ) %>%
  filter(sample_id_std %in% str_to_upper(analysis_ids)) %>%
  inner_join(cluster_df, by = "sample_id_std")

# Count SV events per sample-class
sv_counts <- sv_long %>%
  count(cluster, sample_id_std, class, name = "n")

# Mean per sample within cluster
sv_mean <- sv_counts %>%
  group_by(cluster, class) %>%
  summarise(mean_count = mean(n), .groups = "drop")

# For readability, take top 8 classes by overall mean
top_sv_classes_overall <- sv_mean %>%
  group_by(class) %>%
  summarise(overall_mean = mean(mean_count), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(overall_mean)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 8) %>%
  pull(class)

p_sv <- sv_mean %>%
  filter(class %in% top_sv_classes_overall) %>%
  mutate(class = fct_reorder(class, mean_count)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mean_count, y = class)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75) +
  facet_wrap(~ cluster, scales = "free_y", nrow = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "SV class patterns by cluster (mean #events per sample)",
    x = "Mean SV count per sample",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_sv)
```

```{r}


suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(stringr)
})

stopifnot(exists("sample_labels2"), exists("cluster_assignments"))

# 0) rebuild analysis df (same as your association cell)
df <- sample_labels2 %>%
  left_join(cluster_assignments, by = "sample_id_std") %>%
  mutate(
    cluster = factor(cluster),
    oncotree_code = as.character(oncotree_code),
    meta_top = if ("meta_top" %in% names(.)) as.character(meta_top) else as.character(metastatic_site),
    meta_top = ifelse(is.na(meta_top) | meta_top == "", "Missing", meta_top)
  )

n_total <- nrow(df)
cat("n =", n_total, "\n")

# helper: Cramer's V from a contingency table
cramers_v <- function(tab) {
  chi <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab, correct = FALSE))
  n <- sum(tab)
  r <- nrow(tab); c <- ncol(tab)
  v <- sqrt(as.numeric(chi$statistic) / (n * (min(r - 1, c - 1))))
  list(V = v, chi = chi)
}

# helper: standardized residuals table (for heatmap)
std_residual_df <- function(tab, row_name = "row", col_name = "col") {
  chi <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab, correct = FALSE))
  res <- chi$stdres
  out <- as.data.frame(as.table(res))
  names(out) <- c(row_name, col_name, "stdres")
  out
}

# -------------------------
# 1) Cluster vs OncoTree (use top 12 + Other to stabilize)
# -------------------------
top12_onco <- df %>% count(oncotree_code, sort = TRUE) %>% slice_head(n = 12) %>% pull(oncotree_code)
df_onco <- df %>%
  mutate(oncotree_top = ifelse(oncotree_code %in% top12_onco, oncotree_code, "Other"))

tab_onco <- table(df_onco$cluster, df_onco$oncotree_top)
onco_stats <- cramers_v(tab_onco)
cat("\nCluster vs OncoTree (top12+Other):\n")
cat("  p =", signif(onco_stats$chi$p.value, 3), "\n")
cat("  Cramer's V =", round(onco_stats$V, 3), "\n")

p_onco_res <- std_residual_df(tab_onco, "cluster", "oncotree") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = oncotree, y = cluster, fill = stdres)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(abs(stdres) >= 2, sprintf("%.1f", stdres), "")), size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Where OncoTree differs by cluster (standardized residuals)",
    subtitle = "Cells with |residual| ≥ 2 drive the chi-square signal",
    x = "OncoTree (top 12 + Other)", y = "Cluster", fill = "Std. resid"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1))
print(p_onco_res)

# -------------------------
# 2) Cluster vs metastatic site (use meta_top = Top8 + Other if present)
# -------------------------
# ensure meta_top is "Top 8 + Other" (if you already created it, this keeps it; otherwise we create it here)
meta_counts <- df %>% count(meta_top, sort = TRUE)
top8_meta <- meta_counts %>% slice_head(n = 8) %>% pull(meta_top)

df_meta <- df %>%
  mutate(meta_top2 = ifelse(meta_top %in% top8_meta, meta_top, "Other"))

tab_meta <- table(df_meta$cluster, df_meta$meta_top2)
meta_stats <- cramers_v(tab_meta)
cat("\nCluster vs Metastatic site (top8+Other):\n")
cat("  p =", signif(meta_stats$chi$p.value, 3), "\n")
cat("  Cramer's V =", round(meta_stats$V, 3), "\n")

p_meta_res <- std_residual_df(tab_meta, "cluster", "metast_site") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = metast_site, y = cluster, fill = stdres)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(abs(stdres) >= 2, sprintf("%.1f", stdres), "")), size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Where metastatic site differs by cluster (standardized residuals)",
    subtitle = "Cells with |residual| ≥ 2 drive the chi-square signal",
    x = "Metastatic site (top 8 + Other)", y = "Cluster", fill = "Std. resid"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1))
print(p_meta_res)

# -------------------------
# 3) TMB across clusters: effect size + a compact summary plot
# -------------------------
df_tmb <- df %>% filter(!is.na(tmb))
kw <- kruskal.test(tmb ~ cluster, data = df_tmb)

# epsilon-squared for Kruskal-Wallis (simple, interpretable effect size)
# eps^2 = (H - k + 1) / (n - k)
H <- as.numeric(kw$statistic)
k <- nlevels(df_tmb$cluster)
n <- nrow(df_tmb)
eps2 <- (H - k + 1) / (n - k)

cat("\nTMB across clusters:\n")
cat("  Kruskal-Wallis p =", signif(kw$p.value, 3), "\n")
cat("  Epsilon-squared =", round(eps2, 3), "\n")

tmb_sum <- df_tmb %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median = median(tmb),
    q1 = quantile(tmb, 0.25),
    q3 = quantile(tmb, 0.75),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

p_tmb_sum <- ggplot(tmb_sum, aes(x = cluster, y = median)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = q1, ymax = q3), width = 0.15) +
  labs(
    title = "TMB by cluster (median and IQR)",
    subtitle = paste0("Kruskal-Wallis p=", signif(kw$p.value, 3),
                      " | epsilon^2=", round(eps2, 3)),
    x = "Cluster", y = "TMB (nonsynonymous)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)
print(p_tmb_sum)

# -------------------------
# 4) why chi-square warning
# -------------------------
min_expected_onco <- min(onco_stats$chi$expected)
min_expected_meta <- min(meta_stats$chi$expected)
cat("\nChi-square expected count diagnostics:\n")
cat("  min expected (OncoTree tab) =", round(min_expected_onco, 3), "\n")
cat("  min expected (Met site tab) =", round(min_expected_meta, 3), "\n")
cat("If min expected is very small (<5), chi-square approximation warnings are expected (rare clusters / rare labels).\n")
```
